items.label: Machu Picchu built
items.hint: Abandoned barely a century later

items.label: Great Wall of China begun
items.hint: Qin Shi Huang linked older walls into one

items.label: Colosseum opens in Rome
items.hint: Inaugurated with 100 days of games

items.label: Taj Mahal completed
items.hint: A tomb for one beloved wife

items.label: Great Pyramid of Giza built
items.hint: The only ancient wonder still standing today

items.label: British Empire at greatest extent
items.hint: A quarter of the world's land and people

items.label: Aztec Empire at its height
items.hint: Tenochtitlan was bigger than most European cities

items.label: Roman Empire at greatest extent
items.hint: Under Trajan, from Britain to the Persian Gulf

items.label: United States lands on the Moon
items.hint: The empire that planted a flag off-world

items.label: Mongol Empire at greatest extent
items.hint: The largest contiguous land empire ever

items.label: Cook charts the Pacific
items.hint: First European to map eastern Australia

items.label: Columbus reaches the Americas
items.hint: He died still thinking it was Asia

items.label: Magellan's expedition circles the globe
items.hint: He didn't survive the trip himself

items.label: Marco Polo reaches China
items.hint: He served Kublai Khan for 17 years

items.label: Amundsen reaches the South Pole
items.hint: He beat Scott there by five weeks

items.label: Gutenberg prints the Bible
items.hint: Suddenly books could be made by the thousand

items.label: First steam locomotive runs
items.hint: Trevithick's engine hauled iron in Wales

items.label: First powered flight by the Wright brothers
items.hint: Twelve seconds that shrank the planet

items.label: The World Wide Web goes public
items.hint: Tim Berners-Lee gave it away for free

items.label: Invention of writing in Sumer
items.hint: It began as accounting, not poetry

items.label: Sydney Opera House opens
items.hint: It ran ten years late and wildly over budget

items.label: Eiffel Tower completed
items.hint: Parisians once called it a monstrous eyesore

items.label: Notre-Dame de Paris finished
items.hint: It took almost 200 years to build

items.label: Parthenon completed in Athens
items.hint: Built at the height of Athenian democracy

items.label: Hagia Sophia completed
items.hint: Church, then mosque, then museum, then mosque again

items.label: Muhammad's flight to Medina
items.hint: Year one of the Islamic calendar

items.label: Crucifixion of Jesus
items.hint: The event that splits the Western calendar

items.label: Life of the Buddha
items.hint: A prince who gave up everything to seek enlightenment

items.label: The Great Schism splits Christianity
items.hint: East and West excommunicated each other

items.label: Luther nails up his 95 Theses
items.hint: He just wanted to debate, not start a Reformation

items.label: Storming of the Bastille
items.hint: It held only seven prisoners that day

items.label: Chinese Communist Revolution
items.hint: Mao declared it from Tiananmen's gate

items.label: American Declaration of Independence
items.hint: Life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness

items.label: Bolshevik Revolution in Russia
items.hint: Lenin's name was an alias all along

items.label: Fall of the Berlin Wall
items.hint: Brought down by a confused press conference

items.label: COVID-19 declared a pandemic
items.hint: The world locked its doors at once

items.label: Plague of Justinian
items.hint: It may have helped doom the Roman East

items.label: The Black Death sweeps Europe
items.hint: It killed perhaps half of Europe in a few years

items.label: Spanish Flu pandemic
items.hint: It killed more people than the war it followed

items.label: HIV/AIDS first identified
items.hint: First reported as a rare pneumonia cluster

items.label: Augustus becomes first Roman emperor
items.hint: He found Rome brick and left it marble

items.label: Alexander the Great conquers Persia
items.hint: He wept, they say, with no worlds left to conquer

items.label: First Egyptian dynasty unites the land
items.hint: Narmer joined Upper and Lower Egypt

items.label: Golden Age of Athens under Pericles
items.hint: Democracy, drama and the Parthenon all at once

items.label: Founding of Rome (traditional date)
items.hint: Raised, legend says, by a she-wolf

items.label: Chernobyl meltdown
items.hint: A safety test that went catastrophically wrong

items.label: Titanic sinks
items.hint: The 'unsinkable' ship lasted under three hours

items.label: Great Fire of London
items.hint: It started in a bakery on Pudding Lane

items.label: Vesuvius buries Pompeii
items.hint: Frozen in ash for nearly 1,700 years

items.label: Hindenburg disaster
items.hint: Oh, the humanity!

items.label: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
items.hint: One small step, watched by 600 million

items.label: Gagarin is first human in space
items.hint: He orbited once and parachuted out

items.label: Sputnik launched
items.hint: A beeping metal ball that terrified America

items.label: First spacewalk by Leonov
items.hint: His suit ballooned and nearly trapped him outside

items.label: Space Shuttle's first flight
items.hint: The first reusable spacecraft

items.label: Rembrandt paints The Night Watch
items.hint: It's not actually a night scene at all

items.label: Van Gogh paints The Starry Night
items.hint: Painted from the window of an asylum

items.label: Leonardo completes the Mona Lisa
items.hint: He carried it with him until he died

items.label: Michelangelo finishes the Sistine Chapel ceiling
items.hint: He painted it on his back for four years

items.label: Picasso paints Guernica
items.hint: A scream of protest against a bombing

items.label: William the Conqueror crowned
items.hint: The last successful invasion of England

items.label: Henry VIII breaks with Rome
items.hint: All to marry wife number two

items.label: Charles I executed
items.hint: The only English king put on trial and beheaded

items.label: Elizabeth I defeats the Spanish Armada
items.hint: The weather did much of the fighting

items.label: Victoria becomes queen
items.hint: She was just 18 and reigned for 63 years

title: Turning Points of the American Civil War

items.label: Sherman's March to the Sea
items.hint: Atlanta to Savannah, leaving ruin behind

items.label: Confederates fire on Fort Sumter
items.hint: The first shots, with no one killed

items.label: Battle of Gettysburg
items.hint: Three days that broke the Confederate tide

items.label: Battle of Antietam
items.hint: The bloodiest single day in American history

items.label: Lee surrenders at Appomattox
items.hint: Grant let the rebels keep their horses

items.label: Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species
items.hint: He sat on the idea for 20 years

items.label: Structure of DNA discovered
items.hint: Watson and Crick, leaning on Franklin's photo

items.label: Einstein's theory of general relativity
items.hint: Gravity isn't a force but a bending of space

items.label: Newton publishes the Principia
items.hint: Gravity, math and the clockwork universe

items.label: Copernicus places the Sun at the center
items.hint: Published as he lay dying

items.label: Sack of Constantinople by crusaders
items.hint: A crusade that never reached the Holy Land

items.label: Saladin retakes Jerusalem
items.hint: He spared the city that crusaders had not

items.label: Fall of Acre ends the Crusader states
items.hint: The last Christian foothold in the Levant falls

items.label: Pope Urban II calls the First Crusade
items.hint: 'God wills it!' at Clermont

items.label: Crusaders capture Jerusalem
items.hint: A massacre followed the conquest

items.label: John F. Kennedy shot in Dallas
items.hint: A motorcade, a book depository, a nation in shock

items.label: Abraham Lincoln shot at the theatre
items.hint: Five days after the war effectively ended

items.label: Julius Caesar stabbed in the Senate
items.hint: Et tu, Brute?

items.label: Archduke Franz Ferdinand killed in Sarajevo
items.hint: His driver took a fatal wrong turn

items.label: Mahatma Gandhi assassinated
items.hint: Shot by a Hindu nationalist months after independence

items.label: Magna Carta sealed
items.hint: Barons forced a reluctant king to sign

items.label: Yalta Conference divides postwar Europe
items.hint: The Big Three carved up the map

items.label: Maastricht Treaty creates the EU
items.hint: It gave Europe a shared currency to come

items.label: Peace of Westphalia
items.hint: It ended the Thirty Years' War and made the modern state

items.label: Treaty of Versailles
items.hint: Its harsh terms sowed seeds of the next war

items.label: Chuck Yeager breaks the sound barrier
items.hint: He flew with two broken ribs

items.label: First Concorde commercial flight
items.hint: London to New York in under four hours

items.label: Lindbergh flies solo across the Atlantic
items.hint: 33 hours alone with no radio

items.label: Wright brothers' first powered flight
items.hint: Run by bicycle mechanics on a beach

items.label: First hot air balloon flight
items.hint: A sheep, a duck and a rooster flew first

items.label: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
items.hint: A teenage emperor named Romulus deposed

items.label: Fall of the Aztec Empire
items.hint: Cortes and smallpox together undid it

items.label: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
items.hint: An empire that ended with a signature

items.label: Fall of the last Chinese dynasty
items.hint: A six-year-old emperor abdicated

items.label: Constantinople falls to the Ottomans
items.hint: Cannon finally broke the ancient walls

items.label: Gunpowder weapons used in China
items.hint: First it was meant to chase off evil spirits

items.label: Tanks debut on the Somme
items.hint: Codenamed 'tanks' to disguise them as water carriers

items.label: The machine gun perfected by Maxim
items.hint: One man's firepower equalled a whole unit

items.label: The longbow dominates at Crécy
items.hint: English archers humbled French knights

items.label: First atomic bomb dropped
items.hint: One plane, one bomb, one city gone

title: Voyages of the Age of Sail

items.label: Drake completes his circumnavigation
items.hint: A pirate knighted on his own ship's deck

items.label: The Mayflower reaches Plymouth
items.hint: They had aimed for Virginia

items.label: Cook's first Pacific voyage returns
items.hint: He brought back Australia on his charts

items.label: Vasco da Gama reaches India by sea
items.hint: He opened the spice route around Africa

items.label: Zheng He's treasure fleets set out
items.hint: Chinese ships dwarfed anything Europe had

items.label: Mozart writes his final symphony, the Jupiter
items.hint: His last and grandest, written in weeks

items.label: Stravinsky's Rite of Spring causes a riot
items.hint: The audience came to blows at the premiere

items.label: Bach completes the Brandenburg Concertos
items.hint: The dedicatee may never have had them played

items.label: Beethoven premieres his Ninth Symphony
items.hint: He was completely deaf by then

items.label: Wagner completes the Ring cycle
items.hint: Four operas spanning some 26 years of work

items.label: Marie Curie wins her first Nobel Prize
items.hint: The only person to win in two sciences

items.label: Joan of Arc burned at the stake
items.hint: A teenage peasant who led armies

items.label: Cleopatra dies
items.hint: The last pharaoh of Egypt, by an asp's bite

items.label: Catherine the Great takes the Russian throne
items.hint: She came to power by deposing her husband

items.label: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her seat
items.hint: One quiet act that lit a movement

title: The Road to World War II

items.label: Germany annexes Austria (the Anschluss)
items.hint: Greeted by cheering crowds in Vienna

items.label: Hitler becomes Chancellor of Germany
items.hint: He was appointed, not elected, to the post

items.label: Wall Street Crash
items.hint: The boom ended on Black Tuesday

items.label: Fall of France
items.hint: Six weeks and the Maginot Line was bypassed

items.label: Germany invades Poland
items.hint: Two days later, war was declared

items.label: Babylon flourishes under Hammurabi
items.hint: Home to one of the first written law codes

items.label: Tenochtitlan founded by the Aztecs
items.hint: Built on an island where an eagle ate a snake

items.label: Baghdad founded as Abbasid capital
items.hint: A round city built for the Caliph

items.label: Angkor abandoned by the Khmer
items.hint: The jungle swallowed the largest pre-modern city

items.label: Carthage destroyed by Rome
items.hint: Its fields were said to be sown with salt

items.label: Nelson Mandela released from prison
items.hint: 27 years jailed, then president four years later

items.label: Brown v. Board of Education ruling
items.hint: Separate schools ruled inherently unequal

items.label: Slavery abolished in the British Empire
items.hint: Owners, not the enslaved, were paid compensation

items.label: US Emancipation Proclamation
items.hint: It freed slaves only in rebel territory at first

items.label: Women win the vote in the United States
items.hint: The 19th Amendment, after 70 years of campaigning

items.label: Battle of Thermopylae
items.hint: 300 Spartans against a vast Persian host

items.label: Battle of Gaugamela
items.hint: Alexander shattered Darius's empire here

items.label: Battle of Cannae
items.hint: Hannibal's double envelopment, still taught today

items.label: Battle of Actium
items.hint: Octavian's victory ended the Roman Republic

items.label: Battle of Marathon
items.hint: A runner's 26 miles gave the race its name

items.label: Most of Africa decolonizes
items.hint: Seventeen nations born in a single 'Year of Africa'

items.label: United States declares independence
items.hint: Thirteen colonies break from Britain

items.label: Greece wins independence from the Ottomans
items.hint: Lord Byron died fighting for the cause

items.label: India gains independence
items.hint: Freedom came with a painful partition

items.label: Haiti wins independence
items.hint: The only successful slave revolt to found a nation

items.label: First electronic computer ENIAC unveiled
items.hint: It filled a room and weighed 30 tons

items.label: The first email is sent
items.hint: Ray Tomlinson chose the @ symbol

items.label: Apple's first personal computer
items.hint: Built in a garage and sold for $666.66

items.label: The first iPhone released
items.hint: It put the internet in everyone's pocket

items.label: World Wide Web invented at CERN
items.hint: A proposal his boss called 'vague but exciting'

title: Five Days of the Battle of Waterloo Campaign

items.label: Napoleon abdicates a second time
items.hint: Then exiled to St Helena for good

items.label: Battle of Ligny
items.hint: Napoleon's last-ever battlefield victory

items.label: Battle of Waterloo
items.hint: Blucher's Prussians arrived just in time

items.label: Napoleon escapes Elba
items.hint: Beginning the Hundred Days

items.label: Napoleon reaches Paris, king flees
items.hint: Louis XVIII bolted for Belgium

title: The Final Year of the Roman Republic

items.label: Battle of Actium ends the wars
items.hint: Octavian alone is left standing

items.label: Battle of Pharsalus
items.hint: Caesar crushes Pompey in Greece

items.label: Assassination of Caesar
items.hint: Sixty senators, twenty-three wounds

items.label: Caesar declared dictator for life
items.hint: A title that sealed his fate

items.label: Caesar crosses the Rubicon
items.hint: 'The die is cast'

items.label: Mary I dies
items.hint: 'Bloody Mary', her phantom pregnancy never real

items.label: Elizabeth I crowned
items.hint: The Virgin Queen begins her long reign

items.label: Edward VI dies
items.hint: A teenage king dead of likely tuberculosis

items.label: Lady Jane Grey reigns nine days
items.hint: Queen for less than a fortnight, then beheaded

items.label: Henry VIII dies
items.hint: Leaving a sickly boy of nine as king

items.label: Execution of the Romanov family
items.hint: Shot in a cellar at Yekaterinburg

items.label: Lenin returns to Russia in a sealed train
items.hint: Germany smuggled him in as a weapon

items.label: Bolsheviks storm the Winter Palace
items.hint: The 'storming' met almost no resistance

items.label: Tsar Nicholas II abdicates
items.hint: Three centuries of Romanov rule end

items.label: Russia exits WWI at Brest-Litovsk
items.hint: A humiliating peace that gave away vast lands

title: Six Weeks That Sparked the Great War

items.label: Germany invades Belgium
items.hint: The 'scrap of paper' guaranteeing neutrality

items.label: Archduke assassinated in Sarajevo
items.hint: A sandwich shop and a wrong turn

items.label: Germany declares war on Russia
items.hint: Alliances dragged everyone in within days

items.label: Austria-Hungary declares war on Serbia
items.hint: After an ultimatum designed to be refused

items.label: Britain declares war on Germany
items.hint: 'The lamps are going out all over Europe'

items.label: The Pazzi Conspiracy
items.hint: Murder in the cathedral during Mass

items.label: Birth of Leonardo da Vinci
items.hint: Born out of wedlock near a Tuscan village

items.label: Brunelleschi wins the dome competition
items.hint: He refused to reveal how he'd build it

items.label: Donatello casts his bronze David
items.hint: The first free-standing nude since antiquity

items.label: Cosimo de' Medici returns from exile to rule
items.hint: He ran the city without ever holding office

title: The Cuban Missile Crisis, Day by Day

items.label: A U-2 is shot down over Cuba
items.hint: The crisis's only combat death

items.label: U-2 spots Soviet missiles in Cuba
items.hint: Photos that started thirteen tense days

items.label: Khrushchev agrees to remove the missiles
items.hint: A secret deal on Turkey sweetened it

items.label: Soviet ships turn back at the line
items.hint: 'We're eyeball to eyeball, and the other fellow just blinked'

items.label: Kennedy announces a naval 'quarantine'
items.hint: 'Blockade' would have meant an act of war

title: The Reign of Terror Devours Its Own

items.label: Marie Antoinette executed
items.hint: 'Let them eat cake' she never said

items.label: Marat stabbed in his bath
items.hint: Charlotte Corday's single knife thrust

items.label: Robespierre executed, ending the Terror
items.hint: The Incorruptible falls on Thermidor

items.label: Louis XVI executed by guillotine
items.hint: Citizen Capet meets the National Razor

items.label: Danton sent to the guillotine
items.hint: The revolution devouring its own children

title: The Race to the South Pole

items.label: Scott reaches the Pole, finds the rival's flag
items.hint: 'Great God! this is an awful place'

items.label: Scott's last diary entry
items.hint: Eleven miles from the supply depot

items.label: Amundsen secretly diverts south
items.hint: He'd told the world he was heading north

items.label: Scott's Terra Nova party sets out from base
items.hint: Hauling sledges by man-power, fatally slow

items.label: Amundsen reaches the South Pole
items.hint: First, with dogs and skis

title: The Mongol Conquests of Genghis Khan

items.label: Fall of Beijing (Zhongdu)
items.hint: The Jin capital sacked and burned

items.label: Mongols breach the Great Wall into Jin China
items.hint: The start of a decades-long Chinese campaign

items.label: Destruction of the Khwarazmian Empire
items.hint: Vengeance for murdered Mongol envoys

items.label: Temujin proclaimed Genghis Khan
items.hint: The tribes of the steppe united under one ruler

items.label: Death of Genghis Khan
items.hint: His burial place is still unknown
